hello.handshape: Open B (flat hand, fingers together, thumb tucked alongside).
hello.movement: Touch the thumb side of the hand to your forehead, then move outward and slightly down (like a casual salute).
hello.location: Starts at the temple/forehead, ends in front of the cheek.
hello.orientation: Palm faces left (for a right-handed signer); the back of the hand faces the camera at the start.
hello.framing: Keep your face fully visible; the hand should be next to the head, not blocking it.
hello.common_confusions: Don't snap the wrist like a military salute. Don't tap repeatedly.

goodbye.movement: Fingers flex down to the palm twice (like a small wave/curl), keeping the wrist still.
goodbye.location: Hand held up at shoulder height, palm facing forward.
goodbye.framing: Keep the hand inside the box; don't swing it across your body.
goodbye.common_confusions: This is finger-flex, not a side-to-side wave. The full-arm wave is a different gesture.

please.handshape: Open hand (flat B / 5), palm flat against the chest.
please.movement: Circular motion on the chest, clockwise from your point of view, two to three small circles.
please.location: Center of the chest, over the sternum.
please.orientation: Palm flat against the body the entire time.
please.framing: Hand should stay on the chest, not float in front of it.
please.common_confusions: Don't confuse with SORRY (same circle but with a closed fist). PLEASE uses an open flat hand.

thank_you.movement: Fingertips touch the chin, then move forward and down toward the person you're thanking, ending palm-up.
thank_you.location: Starts at the chin, ends in neutral space in front of you.
thank_you.orientation: Palm faces in (toward you) at the chin, then rotates to palm-up as it moves forward.
thank_you.framing: Make sure the chin start position is visible above the guide box.
thank_you.common_confusions: Don't blow a kiss; the hand should be flat, not pursed. Movement is forward-and-down, not just outward.

sorry.handshape: Closed fist (S handshape), thumb wrapped over fingers.
sorry.movement: Circular motion on the chest, two to three small clockwise circles (from your point of view).
sorry.location: Center of the chest, over the sternum.
sorry.orientation: Knuckles face away from the body; the back of the fist circles against the chest.
sorry.framing: Hand stays in contact with the chest; don't drift off the body.
sorry.common_confusions: PLEASE uses the same circle but with an OPEN flat hand. Keep the fist tight to distinguish.

yes.handshape: Closed fist (S handshape), thumb on the outside.
yes.movement: Bob the fist up and down at the wrist, like a head nodding 'yes'. Two clear nods.
yes.location: Neutral space in front of the chest.
yes.orientation: Knuckles face up; the fist 'looks' forward like a head.
yes.framing: Single hand, held at chest height inside the box.
yes.common_confusions: Don't move the whole arm. The motion is at the wrist only.

no.handshape: Index finger and middle finger extended, thumb extended (like a 3 with the thumb out).
no.movement: Snap the index and middle fingers down to meet the thumb in one quick motion, like a beak closing.
no.orientation: Palm faces down/forward; the closing motion is visible to the camera.
no.framing: Hand should be close enough to the camera that the finger snap is clearly visible.
no.common_confusions: Don't shake your head or wag a single finger. It's a one-shot finger snap.

help.handshape: Dominant hand: closed A handshape (fist with thumb up). Non-dominant hand: open flat (B).
help.movement: Place the A-hand (thumb up) on top of the flat open palm; lift both hands together as one unit.
help.location: Starts in front of the chest; lift to about shoulder height.
help.orientation: Flat palm faces up; the A-hand sits on it like an object being lifted.
help.framing: Both hands must be visible; don't let the lower hand drift off-screen.
help.common_confusions: The two hands move together. Don't lift just the top hand off the palm.

understand.handshape: Use a natural relaxed handshape appropriate for this greeting sign.
understand.movement: Make one clear movement; avoid repeating nervously.
understand.location: Sign in front of your chest or neutral space.
understand.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
understand.framing: Keep both hands inside the camera guide box.
understand.common_confusions: If confused with a similar greetings sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

dont_understand.handshape: Use a natural relaxed handshape appropriate for this greeting sign.
dont_understand.movement: Make one clear movement; avoid repeating nervously.
dont_understand.location: Sign in front of your chest or neutral space.
dont_understand.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
dont_understand.framing: Keep both hands inside the camera guide box.
dont_understand.common_confusions: If confused with a similar greetings sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

name.handshape: H handshape on both hands (index and middle fingers extended together, others closed).
name.movement: Tap the fingers of the dominant H-hand on top of the fingers of the non-dominant H-hand twice.
name.orientation: The hands form a cross/X shape, dominant on top of non-dominant.
name.framing: Both hands in the box; the tap should be clearly visible.
name.common_confusions: Don't slide the hands — it's two clear taps, not a brush.

nice.handshape: Open flat hands (B handshape) on both hands.
nice.movement: Slide the dominant flat hand across the flat palm of the non-dominant hand, from heel to fingertip.
nice.orientation: Non-dominant palm faces up; dominant palm faces down and slides across.
nice.framing: Both hands visible; the sliding motion happens horizontally.
nice.common_confusions: Don't confuse with CLEAN (similar motion). NICE has one clear slide; some teach a single light brush.

meet.handshape: Both hands in 1 handshape (index finger up, others closed).
meet.movement: Start with the two index fingers apart, pointing up. Bring them together so the knuckles meet at the center.
meet.location: Neutral space at chest height, hands meet at the midline.
meet.orientation: Index fingers point up; palms face each other.
meet.framing: Start position: hands shoulder-width apart inside the box.
meet.common_confusions: Index fingers stay up; don't fold them down. Hands meet, they don't cross.

how.handshape: Both hands curved (bent-B / curved 5), knuckles touching.
how.movement: Start with the backs of the curved hands together, knuckles touching. Rotate both hands forward and up so the palms end facing up.
how.framing: Both hands together at the midline; rotation happens in front of the chest.
how.common_confusions: Single quick rotation, not a continuous shake.

what.handshape: Open 5-hand (all fingers spread), or single open hand.
what.movement: Palm up, shake the hand(s) side-to-side slightly while raising eyebrows (facial grammar).
what.orientation: Palm faces up the entire time.
what.framing: One hand variant is fine; keep it centered in the box.
what.common_confusions: Don't confuse with WHERE (index finger shake, not open hand). WHAT uses the open palm.

where.movement: Shake the index finger side-to-side with small wrist movement.
where.location: Neutral space at chest height, slightly to the dominant side.
where.orientation: Index points up; palm faces forward (toward the camera).
where.framing: Single hand; keep the shake small and contained.
where.common_confusions: Don't confuse with WHAT (open palm shake). WHERE keeps the finger pointed up.

who.handshape: 1 handshape (index finger), or L handshape (thumb + index).
who.movement: Index finger makes a small circle in front of the chin, OR the thumb-index L-hand taps the chin.
who.orientation: Index points up; the circle is small and close to the chin.
who.framing: Face must be visible; the finger should be close to but not touching the chin.
who.common_confusions: Don't confuse with WHY (Y handshape near the forehead).

when.handshape: Check the handshape for this concept; fingers extended or bent as required.
when.movement: Use the correct repeated or single movement path.
when.location: Sign in the correct spatial zone (face, chest, or neutral).
when.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
when.framing: Hold the sign still for one second inside the guide box.
when.common_confusions: If confused with a similar questions sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

why.handshape: Check the handshape for this concept; fingers extended or bent as required.
why.movement: Use the correct repeated or single movement path.
why.location: Sign in the correct spatial zone (face, chest, or neutral).
why.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
why.framing: Hold the sign still for one second inside the guide box.
why.common_confusions: If confused with a similar questions sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

which.handshape: Check the handshape for this concept; fingers extended or bent as required.
which.movement: Use the correct repeated or single movement path.
which.location: Sign in the correct spatial zone (face, chest, or neutral).
which.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
which.framing: Hold the sign still for one second inside the guide box.
which.common_confusions: If confused with a similar questions sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

one.handshape: Index finger up (1 handshape), other fingers closed against the palm.
one.movement: Static. Hold the handshape clearly for the duration.
one.location: Chest height, slightly to the dominant side.
one.orientation: Palm faces TOWARD the signer (not the camera); the back of the hand faces the camera.
one.framing: One hand, clearly visible, finger pointed straight up.
one.common_confusions: Palm faces the SIGNER, not outward. (Palm-out 1 is the letter D or pointing.)

two.handshape: Index + middle fingers extended (V shape), thumb closed.
two.orientation: Palm faces the signer; back of hand toward the camera.
two.framing: Fingers spread clearly in a V; ring and pinky tucked.
two.common_confusions: Don't confuse with the letter V (palm out). TWO has palm facing the signer.

three.handshape: Thumb, index, and middle fingers extended; ring and pinky closed.
three.framing: Three fingers clearly spread (thumb + index + middle).
three.common_confusions: NOT index + middle + ring (that's a different convention). ASL three is thumb + index + middle.

four.handshape: Four fingers extended and slightly spread; thumb tucked across the palm.
four.framing: Thumb clearly tucked, four fingers up.
four.common_confusions: If the thumb sticks out, it reads as FIVE. Tuck it firmly.

five.handshape: All five fingers extended and spread (open 5).
five.common_confusions: If the thumb is tucked, it reads as FOUR. Spread the thumb wide.

six.handshape: Form the correct number handshape with clear finger separation.
six.movement: Hold the handshape steady without wiggling fingers.
six.location: Sign at chest height in the center of the frame.
six.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
six.framing: Face the palm toward the camera when required for the number.
six.common_confusions: If confused with a similar numbers sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

seven.handshape: Form the correct number handshape with clear finger separation.
seven.movement: Hold the handshape steady without wiggling fingers.
seven.location: Sign at chest height in the center of the frame.
seven.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
seven.framing: Face the palm toward the camera when required for the number.
seven.common_confusions: If confused with a similar numbers sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

eight.handshape: Form the correct number handshape with clear finger separation.
eight.movement: Hold the handshape steady without wiggling fingers.
eight.location: Sign at chest height in the center of the frame.
eight.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
eight.framing: Face the palm toward the camera when required for the number.
eight.common_confusions: If confused with a similar numbers sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

nine.movement: Hold the handshape steady without wiggling fingers.

nine.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
nine.framing: Face the palm toward the camera when required for the number.

ten.movement: Hold the handshape steady without wiggling fingers.

ten.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
ten.framing: Face the palm toward the camera when required for the number.
ten.common_confusions: If confused with a similar numbers sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

red.handshape: Use the specific handshape for this color (often finger variation).
red.movement: Shake or brush movement should match the color sign pattern.
red.location: Sign near the chin or neutral space as taught for colors.
red.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
red.framing: Do not block your face with your hands.
red.common_confusions: If confused with a similar colors sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

friend.handshape: Both hands in X handshape (index curled into a hook, others closed).
friend.movement: Hook the dominant index over the non-dominant index. Then unhook, flip both hands, and hook again with the other on top.
friend.orientation: Indexes curl into each other; alternating hand on top.
friend.framing: Both hands at the midline; the two-hook sequence is one continuous motion.
friend.common_confusions: Two clear hooks (top-then-flip). Not a single hook.

eat.handshape: Flat-O (fingers and thumb pinched together as if holding small food).
eat.movement: Tap the fingertips to the lips twice.
eat.orientation: Fingertips toward the mouth; palm faces the signer.
eat.framing: Face must be visible; hand approaches from below.
eat.common_confusions: Don't confuse with DRINK (C handshape tilted to mouth). EAT uses pinched fingertips.

drink.handshape: Check the handshape for this concept; fingers extended or bent as required.
drink.movement: Use the correct repeated or single movement path.
drink.location: Sign in the correct spatial zone (face, chest, or neutral).
drink.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
drink.framing: Hold the sign still for one second inside the guide box.
drink.common_confusions: If confused with a similar verbs sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

sleep.handshape: Starts as open 5, ends as flat-O or A.
sleep.movement: Open hand starts at the face palm-in; draws down over the face while closing to a flat-O.
sleep.location: Face — starts at forehead/eyes, ends at the chin.
sleep.orientation: Palm faces the signer the entire time.
sleep.framing: Full face must be visible at the start. Head can tilt slightly.
sleep.common_confusions: One smooth motion top-to-bottom. Don't repeat or open the hand back up.

want.location: Sign in the correct spatial zone (face, chest, or neutral).
want.orientation: Palm orientation should match the reference for this sign.
want.framing: Hold the sign still for one second inside the guide box.
want.common_confusions: If confused with a similar verbs sign, slow down and emphasize handshape.

water.handshape: W handshape (index, middle, and ring fingers up; thumb and pinky touching).
water.movement: Tap the index finger to the chin twice.
water.orientation: Palm faces left (for right-handed signer); the three fingers visible to the camera.
water.framing: Chin must be visible; hand approaches from below.
water.common_confusions: Initialized sign (the W is for water). Three fingers up, not two.

deaf.movement: Touch the index finger near the ear, then near the corner of the mouth (or reverse — both directions are accepted).
deaf.orientation: Index points to the touch points; palm orientation follows naturally.
deaf.framing: Full face/ear in view; don't lean away from the camera.
deaf.common_confusions: Two clear touches. Don't drag the finger continuously across the cheek.
